old_claim.note: Fire Control is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: Producing collagen glue requires hot-water gelatinization to hydrolyze, denature, and solubilize collagen, making controlled heating part of the documented extraction method.

source_shape.source_locator: Introduction explains that hide glue is extracted by immersing collagen-bearing tissues in hot water, where heat causes gelatinization.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: High-temperature water treatment converts collagen-bearing animal tissue into soluble gelatin adhesive.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped claim is production of collagen glue rather than merely finding raw hide, so controlled heat performs the required chemical transformation.

ontology_before: Fire was represented as remote historical background despite the thermal extraction chemistry of hide glue.
ontology_after: Controlled heating is represented as an operating step in producing the identified collagen adhesive.
would_reject_if: The Zurich adhesive were shown to be raw untreated tissue or a cold-extracted binder that did not require collagen gelatinization.
short_reason: Hot-water gelatinization is the chemical extraction step for hide glue.
